channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年06月05日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
item.description: The lower chamber of Congress passed a measure that seeks to halt further military action, in a vote seen as largely symbolic.

item.title: [未译] The Download: China’s brain implant ambitions
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China has approved the world’s first invasive brai

item.title: [未译] Musk's SpaceX share sale: Four things you need to know
item.description: From next week individual investors can take a stake in Musk's rockets-to-AI company.

item.title: [未译] Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
item.description: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

item.title: [未译] Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
item.description: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.

item.title: [未译] Wall Street Sees SpaceX’s AI Revenue 100 Times Higher by 2030
item.description: Research analysts across Wall Street are telling would-be SpaceX IPO buyers that they’re modeling for the company’s artificial intelligence division to see 100 times revenue growth at the end of the d

item.title: [未译] Extension or Imitation? Can you tell the valid Haskell Language Extensions, from the Impostors?
item.description: <p><a href="https://lobste.rs/s/r7by0p/extension_imitation_can_you_tell_valid">Comments</a></p>

item.title: [未译] Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
item.description: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinicsRebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the

item.title: [未译] ReActivaPSOE, la nueva corriente crítica del partido, reclama que Pedro Sánchez no se presente a las elecciones: "Su ciclo político está agotado"
item.description: Urge a que se convoquen las generales este año, "no como cesión a la derecha, sino como acto de higiene democrática" Leer

item.title: [未译] Supreme Court Finds S.E.C. Can Strip Wrongdoers of Illegal Financial Gains, Even Without Proof of Victim Loss
item.description: The justices unanimously sided with the independent financial watchdog, finding that the agency could collect ill-gotten money, even if it could not show victims had suffered financial loss.

item.title: [未译] Lula: ‘We cannot accept the way the United States has treated Brazil this week’
item.description: The Brazilian president wants to negotiate with Donald Trump, but he also warns that, if not, he will seek new partners

item.title: [未译] Gold Steadies as Uncertainty Surrounds Progress in US-Iran Talks
item.description: Gold was little changed as uncertainty persisted over progress in US-Iran talks to end the war that’s roiled global markets.

item.title: [未译] The ancient trick making food waste useful and tasty
item.description: Instead of throwing away byproducts of food processing, fermentation is making them valuable.

item.title: [未译] Single 20-somethings need AI to make first move on dating apps - Hinge boss
item.description: Jackie Jantos says loneliness and lack of confidence were challenges for young adults looking for relationships.

item.title: [未译] Mark Zuckerberg's longest-serving employee on AI, jobs - and her boss
item.description: Naomi Gleit has weathered many controversies at Meta, but remains in what she tells the BBC is her "dream job".

item.title: [未译] Sempra Unit Starts Producing LNG at New Export Plant in Mexico
item.description: Sempra Infrastructure said it began producing liquefied natural gas from Mexico’s first West Coast export terminal, with plans to ultimately ship the fuel to Asia in a move that could add relief to a

item.title: [未译] Thinking Machines’ Murati on AI’s Next Chapter
item.description: Mira Murati, Co-Founder & CEO of Thinking Machines Lab, discusses the future of human-AI interaction, evolving AI products and the biggest opportunities ahead with Bloomberg’s Emily Chang at Bloomberg

item.title: [未译] Cracks Emerge in Kospi’s 105% Rally as Breadth Concerns Mount
item.description: South Korea’s $4.9 trillion stock market is starting to flash signs of strain after a world-beating rally.

item.title: [未译] Here's why election results in the second largest US city are so slow
item.description: It could take weeks - or even a month - before we know who won elections in California and Los Angeles.

item.title: [未译] Asian Stocks to Dip as AI Rally Stalls, Oil Steady: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia looked set to edge lower on Friday as investors grappled with a pullback in artificial-intelligence shares and hopes for a diplomatic resolution to tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: [未译] Oil Steadies After First Drop This Week on Peace Talk Optimism
item.description: Oil was steady after its first decline this week on optimism that talks between the US and Iran will see energy flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: [未译] SpaceX, Other Mega IPOs Denied Fast Index Entry by S&P
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices will keep its existing eligibility requirements for main benchmarks like the S&P 500 Index, rejecting proposals that would have made it faster for mega-cap companies such

item.title: [未译] AI needs a 'brake pedal', warns Anthropic co-founder
item.description: Jack Clark tells BBC's Newsnight AI could get to the point where it develops without human input.

item.title: [未译] Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya
item.description: Plan departs from policy of bringing CDC staff back to US for treatment and offering support to all health workersFormer top US officials and other experts are urging the Trump administration to aband

item.title: [未译] Oscar-nominated Persepolis author Marjane Satrapi dies aged 56
item.description: The French-Iranian author and illustrator was known for her graphic novel series and Oscar-nominated film.

item.title: [未译] Cable lobby warns of chaos if FCC doesn't relax ban on foreign routers
item.description: NCTA seeks waiver from foreign-router ban, citing memory and substrate shortages.

item.title: [未译] Flesh-eating screwworm is confirmed in the U.S., officials say
item.description: The USDA and Texas officials are working to contain New World screwworm from Zavala County, including establishing an infested zone and enforcing quarantine.

item.title: [未译] SpaceX says it's worth $1.75tn as it targets largest stock market debut
item.description: Elon Musk’s space exploration company set a target share price for buyers earlier than expected.

item.title: [未译] Satellite images fuel speculation of Xi visit to North Korea
item.description: The imagery on May 30 by satellite firm Vantor appears to show a fenced-off section and a structure being built in Kim Il Sung Square.

item.title: [未译] Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
item.description: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.
